title: China Conducts Combat Readiness Patrols Near Scarborough Shoal During Balikatan 2026 Exercises
summary: PLA Southern Theater Command announced naval and air combat readiness patrols near Scarborough Shoal, responding to the largest-ever Balikatan exercises involving 17,000+ troops from seven nations including Japan's first active participation.
contents:
## PLA Combat Patrols Counter Balikatan 2026

### Chinese Military Response
China's Southern Theater Command announced naval and air combat readiness patrols near Huangyan Dao (Scarborough Shoal), describing them as "an effective countermeasure to cope with all sorts of rights-violation and provocative acts." The China Coast Guard simultaneously conducted "law-enforcement patrols" near the shoal.

### Balikatan 2026 Scale
The annual exercises, running April 20 to May 8, represent the largest iteration to date. More than 17,000 military personnel from the Philippines, United States, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Canada, and France participate in mock battle scenarios and live-fire maneuvers. Japan's first active participation marks a significant expansion of the exercise's scope.

### PLAN Naval Deployments
The PLA Navy deployed a surface task group consisting of one Type 055 guided missile destroyer, one Type 052D guided missile destroyer, one Type 054A guided missile frigate, and one Type 903A auxiliary oil replenishment vessel east of the Luzon Strait. The aircraft carrier Liaoning transited the Taiwan Strait heading south on April 20, with unverified satellite imagery suggesting operations in the South China Sea alongside multiple escorts. The Type 076 landing helicopter dock reportedly departed Shanghai for sea trials.

### Exercise Activities
Multinational maritime exercises involved 11 warships conducting anti-submarine warfare, deck landing, and other training activities. Chinese vessels shadowed the warships but maintained distance without dangerous maneuvers. Exercises featured sea denial systems including the US NMESIS and Japan's Type 88 anti-ship missile, practiced on the Batanes Islands in the Luzon Strait.

### Philippine Response
AFP spokesperson Roy Vincent Trinidad stated monitoring systems "have not validated any unusual or large-scale military activity" matching Beijing's narrative, characterizing China's claims as "information operations designed to project a false sense of control."

**Sources**: Reuters, ABC News, ISW/AEI China-Taiwan Update, Asia-Pacific Insights
